differentials.name: Crohn disease

differentials.raw: Radiation enteropathy: 1 year post radiation (d)  intramural hemorrhage

differentials.name: Normal =  absence of small intestinal gas / gas within 3-4 variably shaped loops < 2.5 cm in diameter
differentials.raw: Normal =  absence of small intestinal gas / gas within 3-4 variably shaped loops < 2.5 cm in diameter

differentials.name: Mild small bowel stasis =  single / multiple loops of 2.5-3.0 cm in diameter with ≥ 3 air-fluid levels
differentials.raw: Mild small bowel stasis =  single / multiple loops of 2.5-3.0 cm in diameter with ≥ 3 air-fluid levels

differentials.name: Probable SBO pattern =  dilated multiple gas- / fluid-filled loops with air-fluid levels + moderate amount of colonic gas
differentials.raw: Probable SBO pattern =  dilated multiple gas- / fluid-filled loops with air-fluid levels + moderate amount of colonic gas

differentials.name: Definite SBO pattern =  clearly disproportionate gaseous / fluid distension of small bowel relative to colon
differentials.raw: Definite SBO pattern =  clearly disproportionate gaseous / fluid distension of small bowel relative to colon

differentials.raw: closed-loop obstruction of the involved segment (majority of cases)

context: Plain abdominal radiograph (50-66% sensitive): ◊  High sensitivity only for high-grade obstruction! Results:   diagnostic in 50-60%; equivocal in 20-30%; normal / nonspecific / misleading in 10-20% √  "candy cane" appearance in erect position = > 2-3 distended small bowel loops with gas-fluid levels (> 3-5 hours after onset of obstruction): √  gas-fluid levels > 2.5 cm wide √  gas-fluid levels differing > 2 cm in height from one another within same bowel loop √  disparity in size between obstructed loops and contiguous small bowel loops of normal caliber beyond site of obstruction √  small bow
